# Auto Inference

> Agentic CLI for production LLM inference optimization. Drop in any model and any hardware; autoinference picks the optimal inference engine, tunes every config, synthesises custom kernels, and stress-tests the result — so your GPUs run at the limit of silicon.

Auto Inference (npm: `autoinference`, MCP skill: `@autoinference/skill`) is a CLI tool that runs on the **user's own inference cluster** (not a hosted API). The mental model is "wandb for inference": wandb does hyperparameter tuning for training; autoinference does parameter tuning for production LLM inference and gives you a wandb-style dashboard URL per deployment.

## What it does

The CLI deploys a chosen LLM on the user's own hardware with the optimal configuration to yield peak throughput, lowest p99 latency, and lowest cost-per-million-tokens for that exact workload. Behind the CLI, multiple agents form one autonomous pipeline:

- **Hardware probe** — detects topology, interconnect, HBM
- **Engine selection** — ranks compatible engines (SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, tokenspeed, vLLM, LMDeploy) for the model + hardware combination
- **Kernel synthesis** — writes fused Triton / CUTLASS / NVFP4 kernels for the hot path
- **Config tuning** — sweeps batch size, tensor / expert parallelism, KV cache layout, quantisation scheme, speculative decoding
- **Validation harness** — load + correctness + security suites (OWASP LLM 01..10, SBOM + signature)

## Install

The same package ships under three distribution paths so any developer workflow can adopt it:

| Path | One-liner |
|------|-----------|
| npm direct | `npx autoinference` |
| skill registry (MCP) | `npx skill add @autoinference/skill` |
| pip / uv / brew / cargo / docker | per-ecosystem `install autoinference` |

The MCP server exposes three tools to any MCP-aware coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Antigravity): `autoinference.deploy`, `autoinference.benchmark`, `autoinference.summary_url`.

## Supported stack

- **Engines:** vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, tokenspeed, LMDeploy
- **NVIDIA hardware:** H100, H200, B200, B300, GB200 — single-node and multi-node (up to 32-node × 8 GPU = 256 H200 demonstrated)
- **AMD:** MI300X, MI355X
- **Other:** Intel Gaudi 3, Google TPU v5p / v6e, Apple Silicon
- **Runtimes:** CUDA, ROCm, Triton, PyTorch, NCCL, Kubernetes, Docker

## Supported model families (with first-class kernel + drafter choices)

- **DeepSeek V3 / V4 family** — MLA attention, native MTP drafter, DeepGEMM FP8, DeepEP all-to-all, FlashMLA. SGLang is the recommended production engine
- **Kimi 2.6** — EAGLE-3 speculative decoding, NVFP4 fused RMSNorm+RoPE on Blackwell Ultra
- **GLM 5.1** — EAGLE-3 / Medusa-2 drafter, FlashAttention-3, fused norm kernels

## Status

v0.0.1-alpha placeholder — CLI + MCP skill are published across npm, PyPI, crates.io, Homebrew, Hugging Face. The full agentic pipeline ships with the design-partner beta.

## Links

- Website: https://autoinference.org
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/autoinference
- npm (MCP skill): https://www.npmjs.com/package/@autoinference/skill
- GitHub: https://github.com/autoinference/autoinference
- Contact: hello@autoinference.org

## What we are NOT

- Not a hosted inference API. autoinference runs on the user's own GPUs. There is no `api.autoinference.org/v1/<model>` endpoint — that framing is wrong.
- Not a model. Auto Inference does not train or serve models itself; it makes the user's existing inference stack run at the limit of the silicon.
- Not vLLM, SGLang, or TensorRT-LLM. We compose and tune these engines; we don't replace them.
